03/01/2021:
Approximately 370 CS picked up from the Oakdale RST. River Temperature was 10.1 C and DO was 10.82 mg/L when leaving Oakdale at 9:10 AM. Arrived to Caswell at 9:50 AM with DO of 10.65 mg/L and temperature of 9.8 C in the transport tank. All fish were placed in live car and held until release on 03/02.

03/02/2021:
Approximately 260 CS picked up at 10:20. River DO was 11.09 mg/L and temperature was 10.7 C. Arrived at Caswell at 10:59 with DO of 10.27 mg/L and temp of 11.0 C in the transport tank.

Marking Notes:
23 LAD spring-run included in the Oakdale CS pick-up were not utilized for the mark-recapture trial. 3 Chinook were released prior to staining due to poor health. Approximately 60 CS escaped the live car by squeezing through the 3/16" perforations. Recommended purchasing a new live-car tub and perforating with 1/8" holes

A total of 7 mortalities through marking (5) and holding (2), A total of 540 CS were released.

Transport / Marking Notes:

Oakdale (trip #1): 10.8 C / 11.75 mg/L (start of transport), checked fish, 9.0 C / 11.80 mg/L at arrival. 394 Chinook acquired.
Oakdale (trip #2): 11.1 C / 10.69 mg/L (start of transport), 9.3 / 10.99 mg/L at arrival.

394 Chinook acquired on trip 1 to Oakdale and CAS RST fish were held overnight, and the remaining fish (Oakdale trip #2) were included the following day for marking / release.


Release Notes:
Rowed with the zodiak at irrigation pump #2 and evenly distributed / released marked fish across the width of the river using small dipnets (~5-15 fish)

LD, AG stayed @ trap site until 6:45 PM to ensure traps continued to spin.

Shuttled approximately 360 Chinook from Oakdale RSTs to Caswell:
Trip 1 (3/16): 
~270 CS
9.82 mg/L, 11.2 C @ 9:32 AM

Trip 2 (3/17):
~80 CS
11.00 mg/L, 12.1 C @9:24 AM

Marking Notes:
357 Chinook went through the staining process with 9 mortalities occuring during staining and holding.

Release Notes:
348 successfullly stained Chinook were rowed with the Zodiak at irrigation pump #2 and evenly distributed across the width of the river using small dipnets (~5-15 fish). Release occured at 6:55 PM with river temperature at 15.2 C at the release site.

Transportation/Marking Notes:
Hatchery fish were provided by the Mokeulmne Fish Hatchery. Fish were transported from the Mokelumne Hatchery to Caswell via truck (10:30 AM - 11:27 AM) on 2/15/23. Water temperature leaving the hatchery was 8.7 C and the live car containing hatchery fish was 9.0 C when arriving at Caswell. The river temperature at Caswell was 9.4 C and DO was 10.55 mg/L before staining. Fish were marked on 2/15 from 11:50 AM to 2:10 PM.

On the evening of 2/16, fish were released from the zodiak spreading fish out equally across the river using small dipnets (~5 -10 fish) per 30 - 60 seconds.

46 fish were released below the trap before the evening release due to unsuccessful BBY marking. For the next efficiency trial fish will be marked longer (greater than 2 hours and 20 minutes) or VIE tagging will be used.

Marking Notes:
Fish were stained for approximately 3 hours (1 hour longer) due to dye not retaining as well on previous release as well as due to the high turbidity.

Release Notes:
Fish were released evenly across the width of the river by Zodiak using small dipnets (~5 - 10 fish) per 30 - 60 seconds. Fish release occured ~ 2 hours before dusk because high turbidity (20 - 40 NTU) likely decreased opportunities for predation and schooling.
Fish were scattered evenly across the width of the river by Zodiak using small dip nets ~5-10 fish / 20 -30 seconds
Fish were scattered evenly across the width of the river by Zodiak using small dip nets ~5-10 fish / 20 -30 seconds

Marking Notes:
Fish were picked up from the Mokeulmne Hatchery @ 10:30 AM and shuttled to Caswell for VIE tagging. The river temperature upon arrival was 12.9 C and air temperature was ~70 F @ 12:15 PM.

Release Notes:
Fish were scattered evenly across the width of the river using small dipnets ~5-10 fish per 20-30 seconds by Zodiak

Marking Notes:
Started staining with 1.7 g (2.75 vials) of BBY. Added an additional .45 g (.75 vials) BBY at 1 hour of marking. Recommend starting with at least 3.25 vials when turbidity is high (measured at ~20 NTUs). 

Release Notes:
Released fish from Zodiak, spreading fish equally across the width of the river using small dipnets (5-10 fish per 30 seconds). Flows at approximately 1,700 cfs.

Marking Notes:
4 vials total used (2.4 g of BBY) to help dye retain better than previous trial.Water highly turbid.

Release Notes:
Fish were scattered across the width of the river using small dipnets (~5-10 fish) every 30 seconds.

Marking Notes:
Fish transported from Merced River Hatchery through ice chest. Approximately 1.25 hrs with no observed mortality.

Release Notes:
Released fish fromn Zodiak, spreading fish out equally across the width of the river using small dipnets (5-10 fish) per 30 - 60 seconds

Transport Notes: 
Merced DO was between 6-7 mg/L at 12.0 C upon arrival to the Caswell RST site. Extra water from CAS site 10.10 mg/L at 13.7 C was added to the transport tank to improve DO levels.

Release Notes: 
Released from Zodiak, spreading fish equally across the width of the river using small dipnets (5-10 fish/minute) at flows between 600-800 cfs.

Transport Notes:
Bubble box died during transport resulting in low DO in transport tank. Likely reason for higher mortality. Fish recovered in live car for 1.5 hours prior to being marked with VIE.

Release Notes:
Using small dipnets (5-10 fish/30 seconds) by Zodiak (trolling motor) spreading equally across the width of the river

Transport Notes:
Merced 7.50 mg/L when leaving at 9:30 AM. Used two bubble boxes (built ones) to ensure safe DO. Arrive at Caswell RST site at 11:00 AM with DO at 9.55 mg/L and 13.5 C at arrival
